rulings.Case Title: MINGLI WANG VS. STEPHEN WEST MCVEIGH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: FURTHER HEARING RE: MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 2, 2025, line 7. FURTHER HEARING RE: MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. The court's revised indication is to grant the motion but grant attorneys' fees and costs in the amount of $23,098.38 only. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MARY RILEY VS. VANESSA LOUISE GETTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions And The Corresponding Form Interrogatory 17.1
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 2, 2025, line 2. PLAINTIFF MARY RILEY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions And The Corresponding Form Interrogatory 17.1. Plaintiff Mary Riley moves to compel defendant Vanessa Getty to make further responses to her requests for admission, nos. 3-5, 9-11, 13, and 14, and to form interrogatory 17.1. Riley also seeks sanctions. The motion is granted in part. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: FINANCIAL PACIFIC LEASING, INC., A WASHINGTON VS. WIDE AREA, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DBA RC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: NOTICE OF APPLICATION OF WRIT OF POSSESSION AND HEARING Hearing
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 2, 2025, line 5. PLAINTIFF FINANCIAL PACIFIC LEASING, INC.'S APPLICATION OF WRIT OF POSSESSION AND HEARING. The unopposed motion for writ of possession is granted. The court finds that plaintiff has established the probable validity of the claim. The court finds that defendant has no interest in the property after taking into account the amount due and owing on the equipment finance agreement and does not order an undertaking. Moving party is ordered to prepare a proposed order that conforms to Code of Civil Procedure, sections 512.070 and 512.080, send it to the defendant for review (Rule of Court 3.1312), and then submit it to the court for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
